news_summary: India's young tech leaders are inspiring the next generation to embrace AI and innovation enthusiastically.
news_summary_fr: Les jeunes leaders technologiques indiens incitent la prochaine génération à adopter avec enthousiasme l'IA et l'innovation.
news_summary_es: Los jóvenes líderes tecnológicos de la India están inspirando a la próxima generación a adoptar con entusiasmo la inteligencia artificial y la innovación.

articles.title: ‘AI Kid of India’ urges young people to embrace technology
articles.summary: 16-year-old Raul John Aju – dubbed the “AI Kid of India” at home – is a business prodigy who advises government and industry and has created several innovative AI tools.
articles.content:
At the recent India AI summit, Raul told UN News what drives his passion for the technology, and why we need to fully embrace it.

Largely self-taught, Raul built his first robot at the age of 12 and has developed over 10 AI tools. He has advised the Kerala and Dubai governments on how to integrate AI into public services and is the founder of AI Realm Technologies.

Raul’s achievements have led to speaking engagements at major technology companies, and invitations to international conferences, such as last week’s 2026 India AI Summit in New Delhi, where he was introduced to Amandeep Gill, the UN Special Envoy for Digital and Emerging Technologies, as well as UN Secretary-General António Guterres.

After a UN-organised panel discussion on AI and children’s safety, at which he delivered the opening address, Raul sat down with Anshu Sharma from UN News to talk about his business journey, hopes for the future and how young people should best engage with AI.

Soundcloud

This interview has been edited for clarity and length

UN News: Why did you get interested in technology?

Raul John Aju: I discovered technology through video editing. When my father got a job at Adobe I got free access to all the premium tools and I was able to learn about AI. After that I created my robot [known as MeBot, built for a school science project].

© Raul John Aju “AI Kid of India,” Raul John Aju, with United Nations Under-Secretary-General and Special Envoy for Digital and Emerging Technologies, Amandeep Singh Gill, after an ODET side session at the AI Impact Summit 2026 in India.

UN News: How did you come to start your own company?

Raul John Aju: The main reason was I wanted all of the tools that I had built to be under one brand name. I also wanted to create a payment system to make people’s lives easier, so that they can just use a QR code to pay for things, if they don’t have a credit or debit card.

UN News: Tell me about some of your AI innovations.

Raul John Aju: One of them is the Rescue AI tool. The idea is that, if you’re involved in an emergency situation, the tool will tell you what to do, and which laws apply. It will connect you with a lawyer and also help the lawyer to craft a strategy to win the case, by looking at thousands of similar cases.

Another project is ThinkCraft Academy, where you can go from learning about AI to building your own tools to use in daily life. The courses are free, and more than seven million people have been able to learn from them.

UN News: Your decision to hire your own father garnered a lot of attention. What was the thinking behind that?

Raul John Aju: I can gain as much knowledge as I want from my phone, but I don’t have his experience. He has worked for companies like Amazon, Adobe, Wipro, and IBM, so I can learn from him. Also, to be honest, it was good for my marketing!

UN News: You've taught a lot of students. What has that experience been like?

Raul John Aju: I tell them that I want all of them to start using AI but at the same time not forget the human inside them. I always say that the most important skill in the world of AI is to be as human as possible.

© Raul John Aju Raul John Aju at a TEDx event.

UN News: Where do you see yourself in the next 10 years?

I do want to have enough money to be able to get whatever I want for my parents or for myself. At the same time, I want my tools to make daily life easier for people. I also want to spend time in the West and keep on learning.

UN News: How would you sum up your journey so far?

Raul John Aju: I guess I would say two things. First of all, be humble or the world will humble you. Second, follow your passion, not money, because if you follow your passion, the money will automatically come.
articles.source: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
articles.reasoning: The article highlights a young prodigy's significant real-world impact through AI innovations that benefit millions, including free educational tools and emergency legal aid technology. It emphasizes broad societal benefits, youth empowerment, and meaningful advancements in AI accessibility and application.
articles.personality_title: India’s 16-year-old AI prodigy creates tools to help millions
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Raul John Aju, a 16-year-old from India, is known as the “AI Kid of India.” He started building robots and AI tools when he was just 12 years old. Despite his young age, he advises governments and runs his own company focused on artificial intelligence (AI).

**What happened** – At the 2026 India AI Summit, Raul shared his story and ideas. He has created more than 10 AI tools, including Rescue AI, which helps people during emergencies by connecting them with lawyers and giving legal advice. He also founded ThinkCraft Academy, offering free AI courses to over seven million learners. Raul has worked with governments in Kerala and Dubai to bring AI into public services.

**Impact** – Raul’s work shows how young people can use AI to solve real problems. His free courses make AI knowledge accessible to millions, helping many learn new skills without cost. Rescue AI is a unique tool that could change how people handle emergencies by providing quick, smart legal help. Raul’s success also inspires other young people to explore technology.

**What's next step** – Raul plans to keep improving his AI tools to make daily life easier. He wants to keep learning, especially by spending time in Western countries, and hopes his technology will help even more people worldwide. He also aims to balance his passion for AI with personal growth and supporting his family.

**One-sentence takeaway** – At 16, Raul John Aju is using AI to teach millions and provide emergency help, proving that young innovators can create powerful tools to improve everyday life.
articles.personality_title_fr: Un prodige indien de 16 ans crée des outils d’IA pour aider des millions de personnes
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Raul John Aju, un adolescent indien de 16 ans, est surnommé le « Kid de l’IA en Inde ». Il a commencé à construire des robots et des outils d’intelligence artificielle (IA) à l’âge de 12 ans. Malgré son jeune âge, il conseille des gouvernements et dirige sa propre entreprise spécialisée dans l’IA.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Lors du Sommet de l’IA en Inde de 2026, Raul a partagé son parcours et ses idées. Il a créé plus de 10 outils d’IA, dont Rescue AI, qui aide les personnes en situation d’urgence en les mettant en contact avec des avocats et en fournissant des conseils juridiques. Il a aussi fondé ThinkCraft Academy, qui propose des cours gratuits d’IA à plus de sept millions d’apprenants. Raul a collaboré avec les gouvernements du Kerala et de Dubaï pour intégrer l’IA dans les services publics.

**Impact** – Le travail de Raul montre comment les jeunes peuvent utiliser l’IA pour résoudre de vrais problèmes. Ses cours gratuits rendent l’IA accessible à des millions de personnes, leur permettant d’acquérir de nouvelles compétences sans frais. Rescue AI est un outil unique qui pourrait changer la façon dont les gens gèrent les urgences en offrant une aide juridique rapide et intelligente. Le succès de Raul inspire aussi d’autres jeunes à s’intéresser à la technologie.

**Prochaine étape** – Raul prévoit de continuer à améliorer ses outils d’IA pour faciliter la vie quotidienne. Il souhaite continuer à apprendre, notamment en passant du temps dans des pays occidentaux, et espère que sa technologie aidera encore plus de personnes dans le monde. Il veut aussi équilibrer sa passion pour l’IA avec son développement personnel et le soutien à sa famille.

**En une phrase** – À 16 ans, Raul John Aju utilise l’IA pour former des millions de personnes et fournir une aide d’urgence, prouvant que les jeunes innovateurs peuvent créer des outils puissants pour améliorer la vie quotidienne.
articles.personality_title_es: Un prodigio indio de 16 años crea herramientas de IA para ayudar a millones
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Raul John Aju, un joven de 16 años de la India, es conocido como el “Niño de la IA de la India”. Comenzó a construir robots y herramientas de inteligencia artificial (IA) cuando tenía solo 12 años. A pesar de su corta edad, asesora a gobiernos y dirige su propia empresa dedicada a la IA.

**Qué pasó** – En la Cumbre de IA de India 2026, Raul compartió su historia e ideas. Ha creado más de 10 herramientas de IA, incluyendo Rescue AI, que ayuda a las personas en emergencias conectándolas con abogados y ofreciendo consejos legales. También fundó ThinkCraft Academy, que ofrece cursos gratuitos de IA a más de siete millones de personas. Raul ha trabajado con gobiernos en Kerala y Dubái para integrar la IA en los servicios públicos.

**Impacto** – El trabajo de Raul muestra cómo los jóvenes pueden usar la IA para resolver problemas reales. Sus cursos gratuitos hacen que el conocimiento de la IA sea accesible para millones, ayudando a muchos a aprender nuevas habilidades sin costo. Rescue AI es una herramienta única que podría cambiar la forma en que las personas manejan emergencias al brindar ayuda legal rápida e inteligente. El éxito de Raul también inspira a otros jóvenes a explorar la tecnología.

**Próximo paso** – Raul planea seguir mejorando sus herramientas de IA para facilitar la vida diaria. Quiere seguir aprendiendo, especialmente pasando tiempo en países occidentales, y espera que su tecnología ayude a más personas en todo el mundo. También busca equilibrar su pasión por la IA con su crecimiento personal y apoyar a su familia.

**Una frase clave** – A los 16 años, Raul John Aju usa la IA para enseñar a millones y brindar ayuda en emergencias, demostrando que los jóvenes innovadores pueden crear herramientas poderosas para mejorar la vida diaria.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of a young, glowing robot figure made of interconnected circuits and gears, standing confidently beside a stylized open book symbolizing learning, both illuminated by a soft, golden light; in the background, abstract representations of digital networks and simplified QR codes float gently, conveying innovation, education, and the empowering embrace of AI technology by youth.
